message:
feat(lab): add deterministic network simulation for lab runtime

Introduce a lab-mode network simulation layer enabling deterministic
testing of distributed protocol behaviors without real sockets.

New module — src/lab/network/ (834 lines across 3 files):
- config.rs (310 lines): NetworkConfig, SimulatedLink, and
  NetworkTopology types defining latency distributions, packet loss
  rates, bandwidth limits, partitioning, and topology presets
  (FullMesh, Ring, Star, Partitioned) for configurable network models
- network.rs (515 lines): LabNetwork struct implementing deterministic
  packet routing through simulated links with configurable latency
  (uniform/normal/log-normal distributions), jitter, packet loss,
  reordering, and network partitioning — all driven by the lab
  runtime's virtual clock for reproducible test scenarios
- mod.rs (9 lines): module re-exports

New test file — tests/lab_network.rs (50 lines):
- Basic smoke tests for network creation, link configuration, and
  packet delivery through simulated topology

Runtime integration — src/lab/mod.rs, src/lab/runtime.rs:
- Register the network submodule in the lab module
- Wire LabNetwork into LabRuntime initialization so network simulation
  is available alongside the existing deterministic I/O reactor

New file — src/actor.rs:
- Actor model primitives for message-passing between lab-simulated
  nodes, used by the network simulation layer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
